stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{[[k]]\pi} [[c]]\,e^{x}\sin x\,dx \), where integrating by parts twice makes the original integral reappear.
solution: Integrating by parts twice gives \( I = \int [[c]]e^{x}\sin x\,dx = \tfrac{[[c]]e^{x}(\sin x - \cos x)}{2} \). Evaluating from \( 0 \) to \( [[k]]\pi \) yields \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the final division by 2 when solving \( 2I = \dots \) for \( I \).
This flips the sign of the \( (\sin x - \cos x) \) bracket.
This divides by 2 one extra time.
This mis-scales the boundary evaluation by a factor of 3/2.
This triples the correct value through an arithmetic slip.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{[[k]]\pi} [[c]]\,e^{x}\cos x\,dx \), where integrating by parts twice makes the original integral reappear, this time with a \( (\sin x + \cos x) \) bracket.
solution: Integrating by parts twice gives \( I = \int [[c]]e^{x}\cos x\,dx = \tfrac{[[c]]e^{x}(\sin x + \cos x)}{2} \). Evaluating from \( 0 \) to \( [[k]]\pi \) yields \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the final division by 2 when solving \( 2I = \dots \) for \( I \).
This flips the sign of the \( (\sin x + \cos x) \) bracket.
This divides by 2 one extra time.
This mis-scales the boundary evaluation by a factor of 3/2.
This triples the correct value through an arithmetic slip.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{0}^{\pi/4} [[c]]\sec^{3} x\,dx \). (Integrating by parts with \( u = \sec x,\ dv = \sec^{2}x\,dx \) and the identity \( \sec^{2}x = 1+\tan^{2}x \) makes the original integral reappear.)
solution: The reduction gives \( \int \sec^{3}x\,dx = \tfrac{1}{2}\left(\sec x\tan x + \ln|\sec x + \tan x|\right) \). Evaluating from \( 0 \) to \( \pi/4 \) gives \( \tfrac{[[c]]}{2}\left(\sqrt{2} + \ln(1+\sqrt{2})\right) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This doubles the correct value.
This halves the correct value.
This keeps only the \( \sec x\tan x \) piece and drops the logarithm term.
This keeps only the logarithm piece and drops the \( \sec x \tan x \) term.
This adds an extra \( [[c]] \) from mishandling the constant multiple.
This scales the correct value by 3/2.

stem: Evaluate \( \int_{1}^{e^{[[k]]\pi}} [[c]]\sin(\ln x)\,dx \). (Substituting \( t=\ln x \) turns this into a cyclic \( e^{t}\sin t \) integral that reappears after integrating by parts twice.)
solution: With \( t = \ln x \), \( \int [[c]]\sin(\ln x)\,dx = \tfrac{[[c]]x}{2}\left(\sin(\ln x) - \cos(\ln x)\right) \). Evaluating from \( x=1 \) to \( x=e^{[[k]]\pi} \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the final division by 2 when solving \( 2I = \dots \) for \( I \).
This flips the sign of the \( (\sin(\ln x) - \cos(\ln x)) \) bracket.
This divides by 2 one extra time.
This mis-scales the boundary evaluation by a factor of 3/2.
This triples the correct value through an arithmetic slip.
This scales the correct value by 2/3.
